input.initialPrompt: I have some failing tests and want to use hot reload to iterate faster. Can you set it up for me?

data.reasoningText: The user wants to set up hot reload for their .NET test project so they can iterate faster on failing tests. Let me first look at the project files to understand the setup.

data.content: I can see your test project. The express test is failing — it expects `15.00` but `CalculateShipping` returns `10.00`. Let me start `dotnet watch test` for you so tests auto-rerun on file changes:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText: The user has an MSTest project targeting .NET 8. They want hot reload set up using `dotnet watch test` so they can iterate on failing tests. Let me start `dotnet watch test` for them. First, let me make sure `dotnet` is available and then start the watcher.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-f5b77a6d4c3f4a4eb448fcb5785805f1 && dotnet watch test --no-pager 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "dotnet-watch"}

toolArgs.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload
